meta.title: WooCommerce vs Magento: E-Commerce Platform Comparison
meta.description: Structured comparison of WooCommerce and Magento (Adobe Commerce) across performance, cost, scalability, developer experience, and ecosystem.

magento.name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
magento.license: Open Source Edition: OSL-3.0 / Commerce: proprietary

woocommerce.notes: Install WordPress plugin in minutes; guided wizard for products, payments, shipping

magento.notes: Requires server configuration, Composer install, multi-step CLI setup; typically 1 to 3 days

woocommerce.notes: Runs on shared hosting; 2 GB RAM minimum recommended; scales to VPS/cloud

magento.notes: 8 GB RAM minimum recommended; Elasticsearch required; cloud or dedicated server

woocommerce.notes: Adequate for small to mid stores; degrades with many plugins; needs caching plugins

magento.notes: Built-in full-page cache, Varnish support, Redis; better baseline at scale

woocommerce.notes: Works well to ~50k SKUs; high-traffic stores need significant infrastructure investment

magento.notes: Designed for enterprise scale; multi-store, multi-language, multi-currency out of box

woocommerce.notes: Low: $10 to $30/mo hosting + free core; most essential extensions are free or low-cost

magento.notes: High: $500 to $2,000+/mo for Adobe Commerce license + enterprise hosting

woocommerce.notes: Mid: infrastructure costs rise steeply; custom development needed for enterprise features

magento.notes: Very high: $22k to $125k/yr Adobe Commerce license; significant developer costs

woocommerce.notes: 60,000+ WordPress plugins; 800+ WooCommerce extensions; largest ecosystem

magento.notes: Adobe Commerce Marketplace with 4,000+ extensions; higher quality bar, higher prices

woocommerce.notes: WordPress hooks/filters familiar to millions of PHP devs; REST API; React admin blocks

magento.notes: Steep learning curve; module system, DI container, GraphQL API; powerful but complex

woocommerce.notes: Minimal; requires extensions for wholesale, quote requests, company accounts

magento.notes: Adobe Commerce includes company accounts, shared catalogs, requisition lists, quick order

woocommerce.notes: WordPress Multisite works but is limited; each site largely independent

magento.notes: Native multi-store, multi-website, multi-language from single admin; shared catalog

woocommerce.notes: Leverages WordPress SEO plugins (Yoast, RankMath); excellent URL control, sitemaps

magento.notes: Good built-in SEO; canonical URLs, meta control, sitemaps; requires config effort

woocommerce.notes: Frequent WordPress + WooCommerce patches; large attack surface due to plugin sprawl

magento.notes: Quarterly Adobe Commerce patches; Security Scan Tool; smaller plugin surface

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Small to mid content-driven store (under 5,000 SKUs)
use_case_recommendations.reason: Lower cost, faster setup, WordPress content integration, sufficient for most small stores

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Enterprise B2B catalog (10,000+ SKUs, complex pricing)
use_case_recommendations.reason: Native multi-store, shared catalogs, company accounts, and enterprise-grade performance
WordPress is the dominant CMS; WooCommerce slots in natively
Native multi-website architecture; built-in currency and language management
Near-zero initial cost; host on shared for under $20/mo; scale when revenue justifies it
Native promotions engine, customer segments, and cart price rules exceed WooCommerce

magento.core: Open Source free; Adobe Commerce $22,000 to $125,000/yr
